quick_facts.label: Role focus
quick_facts.value: A Primary School Academic Coordinator typically leads and monitors academic delivery for the primary section: curriculum planning, lesson and assessment quality, teacher mentoring, learner-progress review, and communication with school leaders and families.

quick_facts.label: School and board context
quick_facts.value: In a CBSE-affiliated school, academic work includes advance yearly planning, review of pupil progress, staff meetings, teacher professional growth, examinations, and implementation of learning, life-skills, digital, experiential, and value education. A coordinator commonly supports these school-level functions under the principal or academic leadership.

quick_facts.label: Typical preparation
quick_facts.value: This is generally an experienced-school-teaching leadership role rather than a separately regulated national post. Elementary-teaching preparation may include the four-year B.El.Ed. route; current employer advertisements can additionally ask for a B.Ed. and several years of teaching and/or prior leadership experience.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: Compensation is employer-, city-, board-, school-fee-, and experience-dependent. One India-wide school recruitment listing dated May 2026 advertised Primary Coordinator pay of ₹25,000–₹40,000 per month; this is an example vacancy range, not a national salary standard.

sections.heading: What the role does
paragraphs: The coordinator translates the school’s curriculum and academic calendar into consistent primary-classroom practice. Typical work includes preparing or checking annual and lesson plans, monitoring curriculum coverage, supporting assessment design and report-card processes, reviewing student-learning evidence, and arranging responses to learning gaps. Employer descriptions for primary roles also include classroom observation, feedback, teacher mentoring, professional development, parent communication, and academic documentation.

In CBSE-affiliated settings, the work is shaped by broader school obligations to plan academic work, review pupils’ progress, support teachers’ professional development, conduct examinations, and provide learning experiences that include life skills, digital education and experiential learning. For early primary years, familiarity with foundational literacy and numeracy resources and pedagogy is particularly relevant.
bullets: Coordinate yearly, term-wise and lesson-level academic planning for Classes I–V or the school’s equivalent primary grades.
Review curriculum implementation, lesson plans, worksheets and assessment practices against the school’s board and internal expectations.
Observe classrooms; provide actionable instructional feedback, mentoring and training to teachers.
Use assessment and classroom evidence to identify learning gaps and coordinate support or interventions.
Maintain academic records and coordinate communication among teachers, leadership, students and parents.

sections.heading: Common entry routes
paragraphs: A practical route is to first build strong primary-school teaching experience, then take on grade-lead, subject-lead, mentoring, timetable, assessment or curriculum-planning responsibilities before applying for coordinator posts. The exact eligibility is set by the employer and board context, but current school recruitment examples commonly request a B.Ed., five to six years of teaching experience, and at least some prior leadership experience.

For people entering elementary education earlier in their career, NCTE’s B.El.Ed. norms describe a four-year integrated elementary teacher-education degree that combines subject knowledge, human development, pedagogy, communication skills, practicum and a compulsory school internship. Such preparation is relevant to the teaching foundation from which coordinators often progress.
bullets: Develop a primary-teaching track record, including lesson design, classroom management, assessment and family communication.
Pursue an appropriate recognised teacher-education qualification for the school and state context; B.El.Ed. is one elementary-education pathway.
Seek demonstrable leadership experience: mentoring colleagues, coordinating a grade, running teacher meetings, or leading planning and assessment cycles.
Learn the curriculum, assessment approach and compliance processes used by the target school’s board, such as CBSE or Cambridge.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: The role combines instructional expertise with operational coordination. High-value capabilities include knowledge of primary pedagogy and the applicable curriculum; curriculum mapping; lesson and assessment review; interpretation of student-progress information; constructive classroom feedback; teacher coaching; and clear written and spoken communication with staff and parents. Organisation is essential because the role often coordinates calendars, documentation, meetings, assessments and follow-up actions simultaneously.

For CBSE primary contexts, competency- and learning-outcome-oriented planning is useful. CBSE’s principal handbook advises adoption or adaptation of NCERT learning outcomes for Grades 1–10, while CBSE’s foundational-learning resources focus on literacy and numeracy for children aged three to nine.
bullets: Primary pedagogy, child development and inclusive classroom practice.
Curriculum mapping, lesson planning, assessment literacy and learning-outcome alignment.
Classroom observation, coaching, mentoring and facilitation of teacher professional development.
Data-informed review of student progress and planning of support for learning gaps.
Stakeholder communication, meeting facilitation, documentation, scheduling and digital productivity tools.

sections.heading: Where and how the work is done
paragraphs: The role is normally a full-time, school-based position in a private, independent or board-affiliated school, focused on the primary section. It works across classrooms and the academic office rather than being limited to one class: the coordinator connects the principal or head of school with teachers, students and parents. The specific remit varies by school—some roles are CBSE-focused, while others centre on Cambridge or other international curricula.

The workload is cyclical around school calendars: yearly and term planning, ongoing classroom support, assessment windows, report cards, parent-teacher meetings, academic events, orientations and staff development. Consequently, the position blends educational leadership with regular administrative coordination.
bullets: Reports commonly sit with the principal, head of school or academic leadership.
Works closely with primary teachers and may coordinate grade-level leads or subject teams.
May support parent-teacher meetings, orientations, exhibitions, events and school academic documentation.
Must adapt to the school’s board, grade span, language of instruction, policies and internal quality processes.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: There is no single nationally prescribed pay scale for this school-defined role. Pay should be evaluated vacancy by vacancy, taking account of the city, school type, board, grade span, teaching and leadership experience, duties, working days, benefits and whether the advertised figure is fixed pay or total compensation. As a dated market example only, a May 2026 recruitment listing advertised ₹25,000–₹40,000 per month for a Primary Coordinator; a separate employer-provided Vijayawada listing showed ₹30,000–₹40,000 per month for a Primary Academic Coordinator in an IGCSE setting.

Candidates should request the written offer details, including the salary structure, probation conditions, notice period, teacher-workload expectations, leave, provident-fund and insurance provisions where applicable, before comparing roles.
bullets: Treat online vacancy pay as a dated, local indication—not an occupation-wide benchmark.
Compare responsibilities carefully: some “coordinator” jobs combine academic leadership, teaching load, parent relations and administration.
Ask whether compensation includes or excludes statutory and employer benefits.
